description: Polish sweet yeast dough (ciasto drożdżowe) uses enriched dough formula: flour + liquid + fat + eggs + sugar + yeast. Hydration 55-60% creates soft, tearable crumb.

proportions.fat_role: fat coats gluten strands — add after initial mixing, never with yeast
proportions.sugar_limit: max 20% of flour weight — higher sugar inhibits yeast

technique.yeast_activation: dissolve fresh yeast in warm milk (35-38°C) with 1 tsp sugar — wait 10 min until foamy
technique.mixing_order: flour + eggs + yeast mixture → knead 5 min → add softened butter in pieces → knead 10 min until windowpane
technique.windowpane_test: stretch small piece of dough — should form thin translucent membrane without tearing
technique.shaping: punch down gently, shape, place in tin
technique.second_rise: 30-45 min until dough crowns 2cm above tin rim

failure_modes.fix: always test milk temperature on wrist — should feel warm not hot

failure_modes.result: fat coats flour, gluten can't develop, dense crumb
failure_modes.fix: add butter only after 5 minutes of initial kneading

failure_modes.result: poor gluten structure, crumbles when cut
failure_modes.fix: knead until windowpane test passes — 10-15 min by hand, 8 min mixer

failure_modes.result: collapsed structure, sour taste, large holes
failure_modes.fix: watch volume not time — stop at doubled, not tripled

failure_modes.case: too much flour added during kneading
failure_modes.fix: dough will be sticky at first — resist adding flour, keep kneading

description: The richer the dough (more fat+eggs), the slower the rise and longer the bake. Lean doughs rise fast; enriched doughs rise slowly but stay moist longer.
